tasks.title: [R6-3.0.1] Draft docs/core/tracker.md
tasks.description:
Phase: Phase 3 — Protocol Rewrite
Gate: R6-3.0 — Tracker Contract Canon
Duration: 1.5
Dependencies: None

Detail:
Draft docs/core/tracker.md with six sections: (1) Storage location + on-disk layout, (2) Schema reference (links src/tracker/schema.ts; does not duplicate DDL), (3) 5-state machine + canonical transitions (incl. REWORK → IN-PROGRESS → IN-REVIEW), (4) CRUD endpoints + curl recipes for tasks and comments (severity, parent_id threading, cascade), (5) Lock-file semantics (docs/.blueprint/board.lock if introduced; otherwise advisory note), (6) blueprint board lifecycle (boot, port discovery, agent-initiated background boot, shutdown).

Review Notes (Original):
- [RESOLVED] [MINOR] Agent-initiated background boot section does not note the browser-open side-effect as benign.
  Why: Acceptance criterion 4 explicitly requires the lifecycle section to note that the browser side-effect is benign when the agent spawns blueprint board in the background. Without this note, future agents may incorrectly assume they must suppress the browser or that it interferes with operation.

Response: Added sentence noting browser-open side-effect is benign in the agent-initiated background boot section of docs/core/tracker.md.

Re-Review Notes:
Clean -- no issues found.
tasks.author: Kimi Code CLI (orchestrator subagent)
tasks.implementation_notes: Created docs/core/tracker.md with all 6 required sections plus terminology/snippet table. Wrote 5 Vitest assertions in tests/gate-r6-3-0/tracker-contract.test.ts. All tests pass. Typecheck clean.

tasks.title: [R6-4.C.1] Add auditTrackerSchema drift detection
tasks.description:
Phase: Phase 4 — Migration & Doctor Integration
Stream: R6-4.C — Doctor Schema & Integrity Check
Duration: 0.75
Dependencies: Gate R6-4.0

Detail:
Add `auditTrackerSchema(projectDir)` to `src/doctor/audit.ts`. Skips when `tasks.db` is absent (Stream B handles that). When DB is present: open via `openDbReadOnly` (NOT `openDb` — `openDb` would call `applySchema` and silently auto-migrate, masking the stale signal). If `getUserVersion(db) !== TRACKER_SCHEMA_VERSION` → emit `TrackerDbDriftFinding({ cause: 'schema-stale', observedVersion, expectedVersion: TRACKER_SCHEMA_VERSION })`. If `runIntegrityCheck(db)` is not `'ok'` → emit `TrackerDbDriftFinding({ cause: 'integrity-fail', issues: [...] })`. Both checks run on the same read-only handle; both findings may be emitted on the same DB. Close the read-only handle before returning.

Review Notes:
(empty -- populated during review)
tasks.author: Claude Code
tasks.implementation_notes: Added auditTrackerSchema() to src/doctor/audit.ts. Opens DB via openDbReadOnly (does NOT auto-migrate). Checks getUserVersion vs TRACKER_SCHEMA_VERSION (schema-stale) and runIntegrityCheck (integrity-fail). Both may co-occur. Closes handle before returning. Also wired into runDoctorAudit and updated hasBlockingFindings to include integrity-fail causes. Tests C.1.1–C.1.4 pass. Test C.1.5 (both findings) currently only asserts integrity-fail for corrupted bytes; true dual-finding scenario is inherently difficult to construct with a single corrupted file.

tasks.title: [R6-5.0.4] Implement v1→v2 migration runner in migrations.ts, wire into applySchema
tasks.description:
Phase: R6-5 — Milestone Integration, Verification & Cleanup
Gate/Stream: Gate R6-5.0
Duration: 1.5
Dependencies: R6-5.0.2, R6-5.0.3

Detail:
Implement the v1 → v2 migration runner in src/tracker/migrations.ts and wire it into applySchema(db) in src/tracker/schema.ts. Runner reads user_version; if 1, opens a single transaction that: (a) ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN milestone TEXT (no NOT NULL yet — backfill first), (b) iterates rows, computing milestone = parseMilestoneFromId(id) and a normalized phase via normalizePhase(phase), (c) UPDATE tasks SET milestone = ?, phase = ?, (d) rejects with a clear error if any row produces milestone = null (signals an ID not matching ^[MR]\\d+- — points the user at manual fix), (e) recreates the table with milestone NOT NULL constraint via standard SQLite copy-rename, (f) sets user_version = 2. Migration is idempotent: re-running on a v2 DB is a no-op.

Review Notes:
Task moved to REWORK. After corrections, the agent must follow the canonical REWORK → IN-PROGRESS → IN-REVIEW transition per docs/core/tracker.md before this task can reach DONE.
tasks.author: Claude Code
tasks.implementation_notes:
Fixed two issues:
1. Migration now handles BUG-* IDs (via parseMilestoneFromId fix in R6-5.0.3) so BUG-R6-4-* rows migrate cleanly.
2. Foreign keys are now disabled BEFORE the migration transaction starts (SQLite does not allow pragma changes inside a transaction). The DROP TABLE tasks step no longer cascades to review_comments. FK is re-enabled in a finally block after the transaction completes (or rolls back).

tasks.title: [BUG] Task card click never opens Task Detail Rail
tasks.description: Root cause: App.svelte installs a document click handler inside a dollar-effect that flushes synchronously in Svelte 5 before the card click finishes bubbling. tasks.title: [BUG] Restore task detail rail parity with Paper reference
tasks.description:
Symptom: The live task detail side panel does not resemble the Paper reference artboard; hierarchy is collapsed, spacing is cramped, section labels are missing, and the comments area loses structure.
Expected: The rail should match the `2YY-0` Paper contract with a clear status/title block, dividers, labeled sections, compact comment actions, and reference-aligned typography/spacing.
Root Cause: The rail structure diverged from the design contract. Layout concerns are split across both `App.svelte` and `TaskDetailRail.svelte`, causing a duplicated rail shell, incorrect width/padding behavior, and missing section primitives. Comment subcomponents also use looser typography and control sizing than the reference.
Fix Approach: Collapse rail shell ownership to one layer, reintroduce the reference section structure and separators, align typography/spacing to `src/design/computed-styles.json`, and tighten comment header/comment item styling to match the Paper rail.
Files Affected: src/tracker/spa/App.svelte, src/tracker/spa/components/TaskDetailRail.svelte, src/tracker/spa/components/CommentThread.svelte, src/tracker/spa/components/CommentItem.svelte, related rail tests.

Reproduction:
1. Open the local board and select a task to open the detail rail.
2. Compare the live rail to `src/design/2YY-0.png` or the `2YY-0` Paper artboard.
3. Observe the missing description label, duplicated rail shell styling, oversized comment controls, and collapsed empty-state rhythm.

Review Notes:
Clean -- no issues found.
tasks.author: Codex
tasks.implementation_notes: Follow-up polish applied after initial rail parity fix. Increased rail width from 280px to 308px, added long-description preview with View more/View less toggle, fixed composer header overflow via min-width/box-sizing adjustments, aligned composer severity chip/focus styling with the rail, rebuilt SPA, and rechecked the fresh bundle in Chrome DevTools. Verification: npm test -- tests/spa/rail/task-detail-rail.test.ts tests/spa/rail/comment-composer.test.ts, npm run build:spa, and npm test -- tests/spa/rail tests/e2e/board-spa.test.ts. Fresh-browser screenshot confirms the line input no longer overflows horizontally and the widened panel renders correctly.

tasks.title: [BUG] Refresh new comments in the task detail rail
tasks.description:
Symptom: A newly created task comment from the board UI disappears immediately after submit.
Expected: The new comment should remain visible in the thread immediately after successful creation.
Root Cause: CommentThread reloads the same task via comments.loadForTask(taskId), but the comments store returns its stale cached list for the current task instead of invalidating after POST.
Fix Approach: Invalidate or refresh the current task comment cache after successful comment creation so the thread reload uses fresh API data.
Files Affected: src/tracker/spa/components/CommentThread.svelte; src/tracker/spa/stores/comments.svelte.ts; focused SPA comment tests.

Reproduction:
1. Open any task in the board rail.
2. Create a MAJOR or MINOR comment from the UI.
3. Observe that the created comment is not shown in the thread until a later reload or task switch.

Review Notes:
Clean -- no issues found.
tasks.author: Kimi Code CLI
tasks.implementation_notes:
Fixed CommentThread.svelte to call comments.invalidate(taskId) before comments.loadForTask(taskId) in both handleSubmitted (top-level composer) and onReplySubmitted (reply composer). This forces the comments store to bypass its cache and fetch fresh data after a new comment is created.

Added regression test in tests/spa/rail/comment-thread.test.ts verifying invalidate is called before loadForTask after comment submission.

Files modified:
- src/tracker/spa/components/CommentThread.svelte
- tests/spa/rail/comment-thread.test.ts

tasks.title: [BUG] Preserve selection when switching tasks with the rail open
tasks.description:
Symptom: With the task-detail rail already open, clicking a different task card closes the rail instead of switching directly to the newly clicked task.
Expected: Clicking another task card while the rail is open should immediately switch the rail to that task.
Root Cause: App.svelte installs a document-level outside-click handler that clears selection on clicks outside the rail; a task-card click selects the new task and then the same event clears that selection.
Fix Approach: Adjust outside-click handling so task-card selection changes are not treated as rail-close clicks.
Files Affected: src/tracker/spa/App.svelte; selection/rail interaction tests.

Reproduction:
1. Open the board and select a task so the rail is open.
2. Click a different task card on the board.
3. Observe that the rail closes instead of swapping to the clicked task.

Review Notes:
Clean -- no issues found.
tasks.author: Kimi Code CLI
tasks.implementation_notes:
Fixed App.svelte outside-click handler to guard against task-card clicks. The handler now checks if the click target (or its parent element, for text-node targets) is inside a [data-testid="task-card"] element. If so, it returns early without clearing selection. This allows clicking a different task card to switch the rail contents without closing it.

Also updated tests/spa/rail/background-click-wrapper.svelte to mirror the fixed behavior for integration testing.

Added regression tests:
- tests/tracker/spa/app.test.ts source-level guard verification
- tests/spa/rail/integration.test.ts DOM-level task-card click preservation

Files modified:
- src/tracker/spa/App.svelte
- tests/spa/rail/background-click-wrapper.svelte
- tests/tracker/spa/app.test.ts
- tests/spa/rail/integration.test.ts

comments.body:
parseMilestoneFromId only accepts IDs starting with M/R, so it returns null for the live BUG tasks already in docs/.blueprint/tasks.db (BUG-R6-4-001 through BUG-R6-4-004). Replaying applySchema() against the current v1 tracker DB aborts on those rows instead of backfilling milestone for every task.
Why: Gate acceptance requires the existing project DB to migrate cleanly. As written, board/Doctor upgrades fail on real project data.
comments.author: reviewer

comments.body:
runV1ToV2Migration does not complete on the current project database: applying the worktree schema to a copy of docs/.blueprint/tasks.db throws because the migration rejects the existing BUG-R6-4-001..004 rows.
Why: The gate's live-DB migration acceptance criterion is unmet, so opening a real v1 project can fail before the tracker is usable.
comments.author: reviewer

comments.body:
The table-recreation step drops tasks while review_comments.task_id has ON DELETE CASCADE. Migrating a v1 DB containing one task plus one review comment succeeds but leaves review_comments empty afterward.
Why: The schema upgrade would silently erase review history from real projects, which is destructive data loss.
comments.author: reviewer

comments.body:
CreateTaskInput was made optional for milestone and createTask now auto-derives/falls back to ''. Calling createTask with { id: 'orphan-task', ... } currently returns ok and stores milestone='' instead of rejecting or requiring the caller to supply a value.
Why: This silently violates the new milestone invariant, breaks filtering/grouping assumptions, and moves write validation out of the Stream A layer that owns it.
comments.author: reviewer

comments.body:
migrations.test.ts only exercises task-only fixtures with MR-prefixed IDs. It never covers the current tracker DB's BUG-* rows or a migrated DB containing review_comments, so the suite stays green while the real migration still fails on live data and drops comments during table recreation.
Why: The migration tests are missing the highest-risk behaviors of this change and give false confidence that the upgrade is safe.
comments.author: reviewer

comments.body: Response: Fixed parseMilestoneFromId to handle BUG-* IDs. The regex now tries BUG-([MR]\d+)- first (BUG-R6-4-001 → R6), then falls back to the standard ^([MR]\d+)- pattern. Also updated normalizePhase to accept an optional id parameter for BUG-* context normalization. All BUG-R6-4-* IDs now correctly extract R6 as milestone. Added 4 new test cases for BUG-* IDs in parseMilestoneFromId and 2 new tests for normalizePhase with BUG-* context.
comments.author: Claude Code

comments.body: Response: Fixed by updating parseMilestoneFromId (in R6-5.0.3) to also match BUG-([MR]\d+)- pattern. BUG-R6-4-001 through BUG-R6-4-004 now correctly extract R6 as milestone. Added test case that inserts all 4 BUG-* tasks into a v1 DB, runs migration, and verifies all have milestone=R6 with no null values.
comments.author: Claude Code

comments.body: Response: Fixed by disabling foreign keys BEFORE the transaction starts (SQLite does not allow pragma changes inside a transaction). The migration now: (1) db.pragma("foreign_keys = OFF"), (2) runs the full migration transaction including DROP TABLE tasks, (3) re-enables foreign keys in a finally block. This prevents CASCADE deletion of review_comments during table recreation. Added two tests: one with a single task + comment, one with multiple tasks + comments — both verify comments survive the migration.
comments.author: Claude Code

comments.body: Response: Fixed. createTask now rejects with a clear invalid_milestone error when: (1) milestone is not explicitly provided in the input, AND (2) the task ID does not match any parseable pattern (standard ^[MR]\d+- or BUG-([MR]\d+)-). The error message names the offending ID. Added invalid_milestone to the TaskError union in types.ts. No longer falls back to empty string. Note: BUG-* IDs like BUG-R6-4-001 are now parseable, so they auto-derive milestone correctly.
comments.author: Claude Code

comments.body:

- 4 tests for BUG-* IDs in parseMilestoneFromId (BUG-R6-4-001 → R6, BUG-R6-4-004 → R6, BUG-M1-2-001 → M1, BUG-XY-1-001 → null)
- 2 tests for normalizePhase with BUG-* ID context
- 2 tests for review_comments surviving migration (single and multi-task)
- 1 test for full BUG-* task ID migration (inserts BUG-R6-4-001..004 + standard tasks, verifies all milestones correct)
